goal: Produce a one-slide perf budget for the director: current scene cost, RTX 6000 Ada ceiling, and concrete changes to fit two more DCs (28k + 31k prims) in one session.
thoughts: Numbers for a non-technical director — no lecture. scene_summary + find_heavy_prims + check_vram_headroom gives the current-scene baseline + projected headroom for 93k total prims. optimize_scene delivers concrete savings estimates. Honest limits: 'fits in one session' depends on textures more than prims — call this out. 'Why not buy a bigger GPU' counter: even the biggest GPU doesn't fix a 1M-tri-per-rack asset problem. Headless batch changes the VRAM story: no RTX = different budget.
code:
# Director slide. Numbers, levers, honest limits.

# 1. Baseline current DC
scene_summary(stage_path="<DC_STAGE>")
find_heavy_prims(stage_path="<DC_STAGE>", top_n=10)
check_vram_headroom(
    stage_path="<DC_STAGE>",
    projected_additions=[
        {"asset_category": "dc_facility", "projected_prims": <28000>},
        {"asset_category": "dc_facility", "projected_prims": <31000>}
    ]
)

# 2. Apply the instancing + LOD lever, measure the savings
optimize_scene(
    stage_path="<DC_STAGE>",
    enable_instancing=True,
    enable_lod=True,
    preserve_rtx_quality=True,
    dry_run=True
)

# 3. Slide screenshot
capture_viewport(output_path="<OUT>/director_slide_scene.png")

# Slide content (numbers from tool output, not from memory):
#   Current DC:         34k prims   ~18 fps  <VRAM_NOW> GB / 48 GB
#   +28k + 31k:         93k prims   projected <VRAM_PROJ> GB
#   After optimize:     <PRIMS_EQ>  <VRAM_OPT> GB
#   Headroom:           <HEADROOM_GB>
#
# Three real options, with honest limits:
#   1. Per-facility sublayers in one session — works if VRAM budget survives.
#   2. Per-facility sessions on the same workstation — swap by Open. No combined view.
#   3. Headless batch for analysis only — no RTX render cost, different VRAM
#      story entirely (textures don't need to be GPU-resident). Good for
#      overnight SDG or physics runs; no visual director demo.
#
# 'Why not a bigger GPU' counter:
#   - Per-rack mesh at 80k tris instanced still fits on an Ada 2000; VRAM isn't
#     the bottleneck when instancing + LODs are on. A larger GPU does NOT fix
#     non-instanced duplicated geometry or uncompressed 8K textures.
#   - Upgrade only after instancing + LOD + texture compression have run.
#
# 400-AMR reference DC: even instanced, 400 AMRs with unique materials at
# 18 fps current is visibly bottlenecked. Flag honestly.
failure_modes: Inventing a 'multi-DC session mode' that doesn't exist
Claiming a bigger GPU fixes non-instanced duplicated geometry (it doesn't)
Saying headless VRAM is identical to RTX-live VRAM (it isn't; textures dominate differently)
Giving the director a lecture instead of three numbers and three options
